examples: A testator may revoke or alter a will he previously made.
A will needs the signature of the testator and two witness.

One of the most common allegations in estate litigation is that the testator lacked the mental capacity to sign his will.
Of course, you will have to talk it over with the testator before contacting the beneficiaries and explaining what their share entails.
